hypothesis: If real-time commodity price feeds and geopolitical supply-chain risk indices were integrated into the analysis module as dynamic input variables, then the workflow would predict periodic reversals in the economic efficiency ranking of synthesis recipes (e.g., CaO+SiO2 versus Ca3SiO5+Si3N4) that correlate with specific threshold price ratios of calcium-to-silicon precursors, and these crossover points could be identified in advance with sufficient lead time to allow industrial manufacturers to pre-emptively switch synthesis routes and achieve measurably lower production costs compared to static recipe selection strategies.
